justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are substantively populated with multi-sentence analysis. stakeholders_affected lists 7 distinct groups with specific impact per group (far exceeds the 3 minimum). 3 escalations raised for missing information (delivery tier scope, business motivation, SLA contracts). Contractual SLA risk explicitly identified in both the risk field and a dedicated escalation. confidence.level of 52 is well within the <=80 threshold and appropriately calibrated given missing contract information. Competitive positioning is addressed in both financial and risk sections. Assumptions are clearly listed.

observations.text: Exceptional stakeholder breakdown with 7 distinct groups, each with specific impact descriptions — far exceeds requirements
Confidence level of 52 is well-calibrated, reflecting genuine uncertainty about contract terms and tier scope
Three distinct escalations with options and recommendations, covering tier ambiguity, missing motivation, and SLA contracts
Correctly identifies that the industry trend is toward MORE precision, framing this as a counter-trend competitive risk
Edge cases are thorough — same-day delivery incompatibility, timezone issues, API breaking changes, and premium tier degradation
Business rules include the critical insight that changing ETA granularity redefines 'on-time' measurement (BR-2)
Acceptance criteria include transition handling (AC-3) and SLA independence from display changes (AC-4)
Risk section identifies 5 distinct risks including metric gaming — artificially inflated on-time rates masking operational issues
